data.description:
## Summary
The Pass-3 rising-floor novelty scorer feeds the novelty sub-call a prior-findings set that INCLUDES previously-dropped (floored) findings, so a finding permanently dropped for convergence can re-surface as 'carryover' on a later pass — defeating the intended permanent-drop behavior. Novelty must be scored only against findings RETURNED TO THE CLIENT (surfaced blocking/advisory), never against hidden/dropped findings.

## Reproduction Steps
1. With verify.remediation_mode + verify.novelty_drop_active on, run plan-review so a novel + low-priority finding F is floored: recorded in the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar with decision=dropped, drop_reason=novelty.
2. Re-review the same ticket (plan edited, code unchanged) so the floor is eligible.
3. The floor reads prior findings via latest_review_result(ticket_id)['findings'] in _maybe_apply_rising_floor (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:206-207), UNFILTERED.
4. build_payload (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:329-334) writes blocking + advisory + overflow + dropped into 'findings', so the prior set INCLUDES the previously-dropped F.
5. If F re-surfaces this pass, the novelty sub-call matches it to prior F -> LOW novelty (carryover) -> rising_floor_drop (needs novelty>=threshold) no longer drops it -> F is surfaced.

**Expected:** a finding permanently dropped for convergence stays dropped on recurrence; novelty is scored only against surfaced (block/advisory) prior findings. Only inherently lower-impact findings are permanently dropped, and the priority floor is the escape hatch that keeps surfacing higher-impact findings.

**Actual:** previously-dropped findings re-enter the prior set, so a recurring dropped finding scores as carryover and escapes the floor (accidental 'self-healing'), defeating permanent-drop convergence.

## Evidence of inconsistency
The RECALL path prior_concerns() (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py:253-258) ALREADY filters to decision in {block, advisory}; the novelty-floor path does not. The two consumers of the same sidecar payload disagree.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] _maybe_apply_rising_floor filters prior findings to surfaced-only (decision in {block, advisory}) before feeding _score_floor_novelty, matching prior_concerns().
- [ ] Regression test: a dropped finding re-surfacing on the next pass is dropped again (stays permanently floored), NOT surfaced as carryover.
- [ ] Audit every novelty prior-set consumer (attest.py:441 also calls latest_review_result) to confirm none feeds dropped findings as novelty priors.
- [ ] Decide whether the surfaced-only filter belongs in latest_review_result vs each caller (single source of truth).
data.title: Plan-review novelty floor feeds DROPPED prior findings into the novelty sub-call (should be surfaced-only)
